Pod crdb-cluster-2 has the cert mount configured, and the container is running, but the readiness probe is failing with HTTP 503. 

The issue is that:
1. crdb-cluster-0 and crdb-cluster-1 are still running in insecure mode (old pods haven't been updated yet)
2. crdb-cluster-2 started with TLS enabled but it's trying to connect to the cluster (crdb-cluster-0 and crdb-cluster-1) which are still insecure

Wait, but actually the StatefulSet is rolling out. The old pods (0 and 1) haven't been updated yet. 

Since the StatefulSet uses parallel pod management, the rollout happens in reverse order—so crdb-cluster-2 gets updated first with TLS enabled, but it can't form a quorum with the insecure pods still running, which is why the readiness probe is returning 503. The HTTP probe itself might also be failing because the node isn't ready until the cluster reaches quorum. I should check the logs to see what's actually happening, and I think I modified the liveness and readiness probes to handle this transition.

content.thinking:
The key log line is:
```
W260626 03:45:46.454216 5717 gossip/client.go:121 ⋮ ... failed to start gossip client to crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257: initial connection heartbeat failed: grpc: connection error: desc = "transport: authentication handshake failed: tls: first record does not look like a TLS handshake"
```

This confirms what I suspected: crdb-cluster-2 is running in TLS mode but trying to connect to crdb-cluster-0 and crdb-cluster-1 which are still running in insecure mode. 

The rolling update is stuck because the StatefulSet waits for each pod to be Ready before proceeding to the next one, but crdb-cluster-2 can't become Ready while the other nodes are still insecure. I need to force all pods to restart simultaneously so they all pick up the TLS configuration at once—I can do this by deleting all the pods together and letting the StatefulSet controller recreate them with the updated spec. So the key insight is that with `Parallel` pod management, I can delete all three pods simultaneously, forcing them to restart together with the TLS configuration already in place. This way they'll all come up with TLS enabled at the same time and can establish quorum properly.

content.text: The rolling update is stuck because crdb-cluster-2 (TLS) can't connect to pods 0 and 1 (still insecure) — a mixed-mode deadlock. I need to force all pods to restart simultaneously with TLS.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>&1 | grep crdb-cluster- | grep -v openssl)
  echo "[$i] $STATUS"
  READY=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "1/1.*Running" || true)
  if [ "$READY" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
